items.question: What are the two types of IT companies?
items.answer: Two types of companies are there in the IT industry with respect to the development profile. One is Service-Based Companies and the other is Product-Based Companies.

items.question: What are Service-Based Companies?
items.answer: Service-Based Companies are companies like TCS, Infosys, Wipro, IBM, and Accenture. These companies provide software services, and their coding rounds are comparatively easier than Product-Based Companies.

items.question: What are Product-Based Companies?
items.answer: Product-Based Companies are companies like Google, Meta, Amazon, Netflix, Oracle, and 3DPLM. These companies build their own products, and their coding rounds are more challenging.

items.question: What is the first interview round in most IT companies?
items.answer: In almost G5% of IT companies, the first interview round is the Coding Round. It is not the Aptitude Round. Even today, companies continue to start their hiring process with coding.

items.question: Is aptitude required for off-campus interviews?
items.answer: For most off-campus interviews, aptitude is not the primary focus. Nearly G5% of companies begin with the Coding Round, so coding preparation is much more important than aptitude.

items.question: What happens in the Technical Interview?
items.answer: The Technical Interview consists of theory-based questions related to the technologies mentioned in the resume. If the resume contains Java, questions are asked on Object-Oriented Programming, Exception Handling, Multithreading, Spring Boot, and Hibernate.

items.question: Does AI change the interview process?
items.answer: AI helps developers build applications faster, but the interview process remains the same. Companies still conduct Coding Rounds and Technical Interviews before hiring candidates.

items.question: What happens in the second Technical Interview?
items.answer: The second Technical Interview mainly focuses on projects. Candidates explain what they built, how they built it, the technologies they used, whether they used AI tools, which AI IDE or AI agent they used, and whether AI was integrated into the project.

items.question: Why is DSA important for Product-Based Companies?
items.answer: Product-Based Companies conduct difficult Coding Rounds. To clear these rounds, candidates need strong Data Structures and Algorithms knowledge and should be able to solve coding problems from different concepts.

items.question: How many LeetCode problems should be solved for Product-Based Companies?
items.answer: For Product-Based Companies, solving 60 to 80 LeetCode problems covering different topics and concepts provides better preparation for clearing the Coding Round. Solving only a few problems is generally not sufficient.
